PLEASE REFER TO
16-0032
KAREN BO€GLER MCHUGH
OONALD E CASH
STEVEN M. XEN N EOY
FRANCES G. HERBERT
BETTY S. N, AUTON
January 7, ]-99]-
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMI,{ISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 9 58 04 -08 07
Re3 Request for Advice pursuant to Government code
Section 8 3l-14 (b)
To the Commission:
on behalf of city councilman Ed$rard Henderson, I am reguesting
advice regarding his duties and responsibil ities under the
confl ict-of-interest 
provisions of the Politica1 Reforn Act.
The facts which we believe pertinent to this request are as
foLlows:
FACTS
The Councilman has a recorded joint tenancy interest, with a
right of survivorship, in a nobilehone which is focated in a
rnobilehorne park in the city. 
The other joint tenant is the
councj.l,man's elderly aunt. The councilmanrs aunt resided in
the nobil-ehorne untiL she became i11 approxirnately two years ago
and hopes to return to the mobilehorne when her health has
inproved. The Councilman occupied the horne temporarily to
rrhouse sitrt when she left 
to obtain medical, care.
The
CounciLnan returned to the Councilman's own home a feh/ months
ago and the mobilehome is now vacant. The Councilman did not
sign any lease or rentaf agreement with the mobilehome park or
the aunt for the rnobj.lehome park space. the rent for the
mobiLehome park space is bitted soleIy to the Councilman's aunt
and is approximately 9210 per month. The Councilman handles
the aunt's finances and writes the checks for the rent from a
checking account in which the Councilman and the aunt are joint
tenants, but which contains only the auntrs funds.


Fair Political Practi.ces Commission
January 7, L99L
Page 2
In June, 1,990, the city council adopted an interim urgency rent
control ordinance to regulate the amount that owners of
mobilehome parks may charge tenants to rent spaces in
mobilehome parks, vhich was to expire December 31, l-990. on
December l7, 1990, the city Council adopted, by Interim Urgency
Ordinance, and conducted its first reading of a pernanent rent
control ordinance. Under the new ordinance, rents v/iII be
rrrolled-backrr to those in effect on December 31, l-988, plus any
increases inposed bet\.reen that date and the effective date of
the ordinance which do not exceed 66.672 of the increase ln the
Consumer Price Index between that date and the effective date
of the ordinance. Rents may automatically be increased once
every t$/eLve months by 66.672 of the annual change in the
consumer Price Index. Additional increases are available by
administrative applicatj-on and hearing. Under the new ordnance
rents may also be increased in an unregulated amount when
ownership of a home is transferred or a home is removed frorn a
park. This is known as rrvacancy decontroL. rr
There are currently 43 rnobilehorne parks within the City
containing a totaL of approxj.rnately 4,104 rented sPaces. This
represents approximately 308 of the residential units within
the city of Yucaipa. The vacancy rate in these mobilehome
parks is virtuafly 
zero. 
The total number of residential
dwelling units in the City, including rnobilehone spaces, is
approximately L3,483. These figures were obtained by the City
from a report by the San Bernardino county Assessor dated
January of l-990. The population of the city is estimated to be
32,ooo. The City has no figures for the totaf number of
persons residing in nobilehome parks.
Councilman Henderson has excused himself frorn participation in
the adoption of the mobilehome rent control ordinance which is
currently in effect in the City. However, he wishes to obtain
an opinion as to his ability 
to participate 
in future
rnobilehome rent controf matters.
Prior to the Councilrs most recent action on nobil-ehone rent
control , an opinion as to councilman Hendersonts ability 
to
participate in the decisionrnaking was requested from the Cityts
special counsel . This opinion was sought in the interest of
time, with the knowtedge that the Conmission would not have
tirne to adequately review the issue prior to the Councilts
intended action. 
A copy of the opinion letter of Richards,
Watson & cershon is attached for your review.


Fair Political Practices commission
January 7, L99L
Page 3
Please let me know if I can be of further assistance.
Yours sincerely,
BRT'NICK, ALVAREZ & BATTERSBY

sections.question_synthetic: Can City Councilman Edward Henderson participate in future mobilehome rent control matters given his joint tenancy interest in a mobilehome located in a mobilehome park within the city, where he has no direct financial stake but holds property rights with his elderly aunt?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming advice request, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains the FACTS section describing the councilman's situation and the mobilehome rent control context, but no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are explicitly labeled. The legal question is synthesized from the request's stated purpose. Document references a prior opinion from Richards, Watson & Gershon (enclosure).

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Can City Councilman Edward Henderson participate in future mobilehome rent control matters given his joint tenancy interest in a mobilehome located in a mobilehome park within the city, where he has no direct financial stake but holds property rights with his elderly aunt?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the City of Yucaipa's interim city attorney seeking FPPC advice on whether City Councilman Edward Henderson can participate in future mobilehome rent control ordinance decisions, given his recorded joint tenancy interest in a mobilehome in the city.
